title: Reference Rates - Federal Reserve Bank of New York

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York publishes and administers a suite of reference rates for money markets, including the Effective Federal Funds Rate (EFFR), Overnight Bank Funding Rate (OBFR), Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR), Broad General Collateral Rate (BGCR), and Tri-Party General Collateral Rate (TGCR). These reference rates serve market infrastructure functions including facilitating standardized contract trading, reducing information asymmetries, and limiting incentives for price manipulation. The NY Fed has produced reference rates since the 1950s and maintains IOSCO compliance for all administered rates.
key_points: The NY Fed has served as administrator and producer of reference rates since the 1950s, beginning with the daily effective federal funds rate
Reference rates facilitate trading in standardized contracts, reduce information asymmetries, and provide transparent independent pricing sources
The EFFR is calculated from overnight federal funds transaction data reported by domestic and foreign bank branches in the FR 2420 Report
The OBFR expands on EFFR by additionally incorporating overnight Eurodollar transactions and certain deposit transactions at domestic branches
SOFR is calculated based on BGCR data plus transactions cleared through FICC's Delivery-versus-Payment repo service
The NY Fed issued a statement on March 12, 2026 regarding publication schedule for April 3, 2026
why_it_matters: Reference rates produced by the NY Fed form the backbone of trillions of dollars in financial contracts and monetary policy implementation. SOFR has become the primary replacement for LIBOR in many markets. Understanding these rates is essential for evaluating money market dynamics and the transmission of monetary policy to broader financial markets.
market_structure_context: This page describes foundational market infrastructure for reference rates. The EFFR reflects unsecured overnight federal funds transactions, OBFR adds Eurodollar transactions and deposits, SOFR measures the cost of financing Treasury securities overnight (broadest collateralized measure), BGCR covers Treasury general collateral repo, and TGCR covers tri-party Treasury GC repo. These rates collectively inform the pricing of trillions in contracts and serve as benchmarks for evaluating monetary policy transmission.
